LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GREGG HOOD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS DENIED. MR. HOOD'S DECLARATION DOES NOT PROVIDE ANY FACTS TO SUPPORT THE CONCLUSION THAT HE LACKED TIMELY ACTUAL NOTICE (WHICH IS BELIED BY THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF THE SUMMONS) OR THAT THERE IS ANY BASIS TO FIND EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN #6185.

calendar.matters:
MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Feb-14-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GREGG HOOD'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS Denied. Mr. Hood's declaration does not provide any facts to support the conclusion that he lacked timely actual notice (which is belied by the proof of service of the summons) or that there is any basis to find excusable neglect. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.
